title: fix(mcp): add SIGHUP handler to clean up stdio child processes on terminal disconnect
summary: Adds a SIGHUP signal handler to InternalMastraMCPClient alongside the existing SIGTERM handler. When a terminal session closes (tmux, SSH, terminal emulator), the OS sends SIGHUP which was not handled, causing stdio child processes to leak. The fix mirrors the existing SIGTERM pattern: registers a handler on connect() that calls gracefulExit(), and removes it on disconnect(). Includes 3 well-written tests covering listener cleanup on disconnect, no accumulation across connect/disconnect cycles, and no duplicate listeners on repeated connects.
maintainerNotes: Clean, well-scoped fix from external contributor. The implementation exactly mirrors the existing SIGTERM handler pattern. LekoArts (maintainer) has engaged in comments helping with changeset issues but hasn't left a formal review yet. The changeset was updated per CodeRabbit feedback to be user-facing. CI tests are pending (waiting for secrets-gated jobs to run). Vercel deploy check fails because external contributor needs authorization — this is expected and not a blocker. The linked issue #13973 provides good reproduction details. No similar PRs found.
